content: A general-purpose agent’s persistent, self-directed exploration of open-ended environments can be reframed as a nested network of local Intrinsic Curiosity Modules (ICMs), where each sub-module assigned to an unstructured memory cluster generates intrinsic reward for reducing prediction error on that cluster, rather than only rewarding exploration of novel environmental states. This decentralized curiosity architecture automatically prioritizes high-uncertainty gaps in the agent’s active memory that are most relevant to its long-term goals, eliminating the need for manually curated memory pruning schedules.
